title: Sprint 2 — Match Reliability & Error Resilience Issues
goals: Zero silent failures in reward persistence
No dangling realtime subscriptions on dispose
Match state migration path to Freezed evaluated and executed
Tournament match socket logic extracted from 1350-line widget
Dead patch file and unused state machine removed or wired in

stories.title: Surface reward-persistence errors instead of swallowing them
stories.userStory: As a player, I want failures to be visible so that the team can fix them instead of losing my coins and XP.
acceptanceCriteria: MatchCompletionHandler.persistRewards replaces empty catch(_){} with explicit error types (PostgrestException, SocketException, FormatException)
Failures are surfaced via currentUserProvider error state or a dedicated snack/message
Local state update (coins/XP/xp) is NOT persisted when DB write fails; user sees a retry prompt
Root PATCH_match_provider_logging.dart is either removed or converted into the canonical implementation
stories.technicalNotes: Remove empty catch(_){} in match_completion_handler.dart lines 82 and 104. Do not silently strip exceptions.
edgeCases: RPC returns null result but no exception
Fetch fail for getCurrentUser fallback — must guard double-counting
Network timeout on slow mobile connections

stories.title: Unsubscribe realtime channels when providers dispose
stories.userStory: As a user leaving a screen, I want subscriptions to stop so that the app does not leak listeners or receive stale updates.
acceptanceCriteria: CurrentUserNotifier unsubscribes its user channel in dispose when session is null
TeamProvider/MarketProvider expose a refresh/dispose that calls channel.unsubscribe()
No listeners remain for offline users after logout
stories.technicalNotes: SupabaseService.subscribeToUser, subscribeToUserCards, subscribeToSquad all return RealtimeChannel. Call unsubscribe() in corresponding Provider dispose.
edgeCases: Subscription failure after init (channel was never fully joined)

stories.title: Migrate MatchState to Freezed and evaluate MatchStateMachine
stories.userStory: As a maintainer, I want immutable match state and explicit phase transitions so that bugs are caught at compile time.
acceptanceCriteria: MatchState in match_state.dart becomes a @freezed class with copyWith and equality
BatsmanStats and BowlerStats remain mutable or are also migrated as freezed
MatchStateMachine is either wired into MatchState (phase enum) or removed from the codebase
flutter analyze reports 0 issues and existing tests pass
stories.technicalNotes: Route is already built. Implement migration in a single step so Reviewers can evaluate rollback cost. Do not touch screens first.
edgeCases: Existing MatchSummary copy semantics must be preserved
Performance-sensitive copyWith in live match loop must remain O(1)

stories.title: Extract tournament match socket logic from 1350-line widget
stories.userStory: As a future contributor, I want the tournament match screen to be under 300 lines so that I can understand and change it without fear.
acceptanceCriteria: TournamentMatchScreen is thinner than 300 lines and delegates to a testable MatchSocketManager
Commentary parsing is unit-tested (JSON → _TCommentaryEntry list)
State sync from _onRoomJoined covers all fields including homeBatsFirst
No change to user-visible tournament match behavior
stories.technicalNotes: Create lib/providers/match/tournament_match_manager.dart. Move socket lifecycle, state decoding, stats parsing into that manager. Screen subscribes to state updates.
edgeCases: Completed match still needs commentary loading

stories.title: Create a single socket owner and stop double-initializing
stories.userStory: As a developer, I want a single socket owner so that I never have two connection attempts racing in the same app session.
acceptanceCriteria: All socket init paths funnel through a single SocketManager singleton (NodeBackendService)
NodeBackendService.initSocket is truly idempotent — no stale socket leaks
waitForConnection handles already-connected case without redundant timers
No test in the suite can produce two simultaneous Socket.IO connections
stories.technicalNotes: Remove the ad-hoc socket behavior from TournamentMatchScreen. It must call NodeBackendService only.
edgeCases: Socket connected then server closes before first emit

stories.title: Add userId filter to squad_players realtime subscription
stories.userStory: As a user, I want the app to receive only my squad updates so that I do not see every other user's team changes.
acceptanceCriteria: subscribeToSquad includes a user_id EQ filter
Subscription docs/comments updated to explain the filter
No raw squad_players updates enter the app without ownership check
stories.technicalNotes: Currently: .onPostgresChanges(table: 'squad_players') with no filter. Add PostgresChangeFilter eq user_id.
edgeCases: User with no active squad — should not crash on filter setup
Test RLS coverage against this filter

stories.title: Strip or adopt hardcoded backend URL and Supabase key
stories.userStory: As an ops engineer, I want secrets and env URLs out of git-tracked files so that rotating a key does not require a code deploy.
acceptanceCriteria: Env switching is done via --dart-define=SUPABASE_URL / SUPABASE_ANON_KEY only
DefaultValues in main.dart are removed or fed by build-time env
AppConfig.backendUrl reads from a single runtime source
CI note documents required dart-define arguments
stories.technicalNotes: Do NOT paste secrets back. Replace with placeholders and references to dart-define.
edgeCases: Flutter web build passes env via build
